# Transcrição: Two_buckets_of_people_who_inspire_you

- **Fonte:** Two_buckets_of_people_who_inspire_you
- **Duração:** 01:15
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 01:15]

There's two buckets of people that are going to inspire you. People look at Elon Musk, they look at Jeff Bezos, they look at Oprah Winfrey, they look at Brunet Brown. That's people who are 100 steps ahead of you. Those kind of people are really important because they create inspiration. Like Michael Jordan, wow, what an inspiration, but they're so far away from you that if you had a conversation with them, because they are so far ahead of you, when they start giving you thoughts or advice, it's so far removed from where you currently are that it's very difficult for you to apply some of that advice. But then when I look for pragmatic advice, I find people who are only one to two steps ahead of me. So then I go on YouTube, I search on Google, and I look for people who are just a couple of steps ahead of me and I listen to their podcasts. I read their books if they have one, I check out their blogs, I follow them on all social media platforms. I watch what they're doing. And from doing that research and doing that kind of analysis, I then can extract the pragmatic blueprint that is super relevant to my business, super relevant to me currently in my phase of business. And then I'm able to find 10 to 20 moves that are extremely powerful and pragmatic that create immediate results. I used to try to find pragmatic strategies from Elon Musk, and it just doesn't work because he's so far ahead of me in a completely different industry. By Twitter. You know, so it's one of those things where I think it's important to be very conscious of those two buckets.
